  have h : ∃ d : ℕ, ∀ (C : Set (Fin (n + 1) → ℝ)) (hC : Convex ℝ C) (x : Fin n → ℕ → Fin (n + 1) → ℝ)
      (y : Set (Fin n → ℕ) → Fin (n + 1) → ℝ) (hxy : ∀ i s, ∑ k, x k (i k) + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s),
        False := by
    by_cases hn : n = 0
    · -- Case n = 0
      subst hn
      -- When n = 0, the domain of i is Fin 0 → ℕ, which is a singleton set.

      -- The hypothesis hxy will lead to a contradiction because it requires checking being in the empty set.

      intro C hC x y hxy
      have h₁ := hxy (fun i => by
        exfalso
        fin_cases i <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
      ) ∅
      simp at h₁
      <;>
      (try contradiction) <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
      (try {
        have h₂ := hxy (fun i => by
          exfalso
          fin_cases i <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
        ) { (fun i => by

          fin_cases i <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
        ) }
        simp at h₂
        <;>
        simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
        <;>
        (try contradiction)
      }) <;>
      (try {
        have h₂ := hxy (fun i => by
          exfalso
          fin_cases i <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
        ) { (fun i => by
          exfalso
          fin_cases i <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
        ) }
        simp at h₂
        <;>
        simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
        <;>
        (try contradiction)
      })
    · -- Case n ≠ 0
      -- For n ≠ 0, we can still find a contradiction by considering the fact that the cardinality of the set of functions i : Fin n → ℕ is infinite, whereas the conditions imposed by hxy would require checking being in subsets of it, which cannot all be satisfied simultaneously by a convex set C.
      use 0
      intro C hC x y hxy
      have h₁ : n ≠ 0 := hn
      -- For simplicity, we will use the fact that there are at least two distinct functions i : Fin n → ℕ (since n ≠ 0, Fin n is non-empty, and ℕ is infinite).
      -- Specifically, we can take two constant functions with different values.
      -- We will use hxy to derive a contradiction by considering the membership of these two functions in the empty set and their sum with y s in C.
      have h₂ : n > 0 := by
        have h₃ : n ≠ 0 := hn
        have h₄ : n > 0 := Nat.pos_of_ne_zero hn
        exact h₄
      -- Use the first element of Fin n to define constant functions
      have h₃ : ∃ (i j : Fin n → ℕ), i ≠ j := by
        -- Since n > 0, Fin n is non-empty. We can take two distinct constant functions.
        use fun _ => 0, fun _ => 1
        intro h
        have h₄ : (Fin.last 0 : Fin n) = (Fin.last 0 : Fin n) := rfl
        have h₅ := congr_fun h (Fin.last 0)
        simp at h₅
        <;>
        (try contradiction)
        <;>
        (try omega)
      obtain ⟨i, j, hij⟩ := h₃
      have h₄ := hxy i ∅
      have h₅ := hxy j ∅
      have h₆ := hxy i {i}
      have h₇ := hxy j {i}
      have h₈ := hxy i {j}
      have h₉ := hxy j {j}
      -- Simplify the conditions obtained from hxy
      simp [Set.mem_empty_iff_false, iff_false_iff] at h₄ h₅
      simp [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h₆ h₇ h₈ h₉
      <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
        <;>
        (try omega)
        <;>
        (try contradiction)
      }) <;>
      (try {
        have h₁₀ := hxy (fun _ => 0) ∅
        have h₁₁ := hxy (fun _ => 1) ∅
        simp [Set.mem_empty_iff_false, iff_false_iff] at h₁₀ h₁₁
        <;>
        (try {
          simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
          <;>
          (try omega)
          <;>
          (try contradiction)
        })
      })
      <;>
      (try {
        have h₁₀ := hxy i ∅
        have h₁₁ := hxy j ∅
        simp [Set.mem_empty_iff_false, iff_false_iff] at h₁₀ h₁₁
        <;>
        (try {
          simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
          <;>
          (try omega)
          <;>
          (try contradiction)
        })
      })
      <;>
      (try {
        have h₁₀ := hxy i {i}
        have h₁₁ := hxy j {i}
        have h₁₂ := hxy i {j}
        have h₁₃ := hxy j {j}
        simp [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h₁₀ h₁₁ h₁₂ h₁₃
        <;>

          <;>

        })
      })
      <;>

        <;>
        (try omega)
        <;>
        (try contradiction)
      })
  -- We can always choose d = 0 (as in the cases above)
  exact h

  use 0
  intro C hC x y hxy
  have h₁ : (∑ k : Fin n, x k 0) + y (∅ : Set (Fin n → ℕ)) ∈ C ↔ (0 : Fin n → ℕ) ∈ (∅ : Set (Fin n → ℕ)) := by
    have h₂ := hxy (0 : Fin n → ℕ) (∅ : Set (Fin n → ℕ))
    simpa [Function.funext_iff] using h₂
  have h₃ : (∑ k : Fin n, x k 0) + y (∅ : Set (Fin n → ℕ)) ∈ C → False := by
    intro h
    simp at h₁
    <;> simp_all [Set.mem_empty_iff_false]
  have h₄ : (0 : Fin n → ℕ) ∈ (∅ : Set (Fin n → ℕ)) → False := by
    intro h
    simp at h
  have h₅ : (∑ k : Fin n, x k 0) + y (∅ : Set (Fin n → ℕ)) ∈ C ∨ ¬(∑ k : Fin n, x k 0) + y (∅ : Set (Fin n → ℕ)) ∈ C := by
    by_cases h : (∑ k : Fin n, x k 0) + y (∅ : Set (Fin n → ℕ)) ∈ C
    · exact Or.inl h
    · exact Or.inr h
  cases h₅ with
  | inl h₅ =>
    exact h₃ h₅
  | inr h₅ =>
    have h₆ := hxy (0 : Fin n → ℕ) (∅ : Set (Fin n → ℕ))
    simp_all [Set.mem_empty_iff_false]

by
  have h_main : ∀ (C : Set (Fin (n + 1) → ℝ)) (hC : Convex ℝ C) (x : Fin n → ℕ → Fin (n + 1) → ℝ)
      (y : Set (Fin n → ℕ) → Fin (n + 1) → ℝ) (hxy : ∀ i s, ∑ k, x k (i k) + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s),
        False := by
    intro C hC x y hxy
    -- Assume n = 0 for simplicity and contradiction
    have h₁ : False := by
      by_cases hn : n = 0
      · -- Case n = 0: Fin n is empty
        subst hn
        have h₂ := hxy (fun i => 0) (∅ : Set (Fin 0 → ℕ))
        have h₃ := hxy (fun i => 0) ({(fun i => 0)} : Set (Fin 0 → ℕ))
        simp [Fin.sum_univ_zero] at h₂ h₃
        <;> simp_all [Set.mem_singleton_iff]
        <;> aesop
      · -- Case n > 0: Derive a contradiction from the given conditions
        have h₂ := hxy (fun i => 0) (∅ : Set (Fin n → ℕ))
        have h₃ := hxy (fun i => 0) ({(fun i => 0)} : Set (Fin n → ℕ))
        simp [Fin.sum_univ_zero] at h₂ h₃
        <;> simp_all [Set.mem_singleton_iff]
        <;>
          (try {
            have h₄ := hC
            simp [Convex] at h₄
            <;>
              (try { exfalso; aesop })
          })
        <;>
          (try { exfalso; aesop })
        <;>
          (try {
            have h₅ := hxy (fun i => 0) (∅ : Set (Fin n → ℕ))
            have h₆ := hxy (fun i => 0) ({(fun i => 0)} : Set (Fin n → ℕ))
            simp [Fin.sum_univ_zero] at h₅ h₆
            <;> simp_all [Set.mem_singleton_iff]
            <;> aesop
          })
        <;>
          (try {
            have h₅ := hxy (fun i => 0) (∅ : Set (Fin n → ℕ))
            have h₆ := hxy (fun i => 0) ({(fun i => 0)} : Set (Fin n → ℕ))
            simp [Fin.sum_univ_zero] at h₅ h₆
            <;> simp_all [Set.mem_singleton_iff]
            <;> aesop
          })
        <;>
          (try { exfalso; aesop })
        <;>
          (try { contradiction })
    exact h₁
  refine' ⟨0, _⟩
  intro C hC x y hxy
  exact h_main C hC x y hxy
